pattern: Simulate the push/pop sequence with a stack: push elements and greedily pop whenever the stack top matches the next expected pop value.
hints: Use an explicit stack to simulate. Push values from pushed in order; after each push, immediately pop as many times as possible while the stack top equals the current popped target.
Advance a pointer into popped each time you successfully pop. The sequence is valid if and only if the stack is empty after processing all pushed values.
No backtracking is needed: the greedy strategy of popping as early as possible is always optimal.

edge_inputs.description: push all then pop all in reverse — LIFO order, valid
invalid: 1 cannot be popped before 2 since 2 was pushed later but pops first here
valid sequence with early pop then continue
out-of-order pop that can never be achieved
all pushes before any pop, valid reverse order

stress.description: random valid push/pop permutation of size 1000
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    pushed = list(range(n))
    rng.shuffle(pushed)
    # generate a valid pop sequence by simulating
    stack = []
    popped = []
    pi = 0
    for v in pushed:
        stack.append(v)
        while stack and rng.random() < 0.5:
            popped.append(stack.pop())
    while stack:
        popped.append(stack.pop())
    return [pushed, popped]
